levels.label: A1 — Beginner
levels.grammar_notes: Use only simple present tense (am, is, are, have, like, want). Basic vocabulary only. Maximum 8 words per sentence. No idioms. No contractions in questions.

levels.label: A2 — Elementary
levels.grammar_notes: Use simple present and simple past. Can/could for ability and requests. Basic connectors: and, but, because, so. Common everyday vocabulary. Sentences up to 15 words.

levels.label: B1 — Intermediate
levels.grammar_notes: Use present, past, present perfect, going to/will. Relative clauses (who, which, that). Connectors: although, however, in order to, because of. Express opinions and make suggestions naturally.

levels.label: B2 — Upper Intermediate
levels.grammar_notes: Full range of common tenses. First and second conditionals. Passive voice. Wide vocabulary. Complex sentences with multiple clauses. Phrasal verbs in context.

levels.label: C1 — Advanced
levels.grammar_notes: Full grammar range including third conditional, subjunctive, and inversion. Idiomatic expressions, collocations, and sophisticated vocabulary. Natural native-like sentence rhythm.

levels.label: C2 — Mastery
levels.grammar_notes: All grammar structures including rare and literary forms. Rich vocabulary, cultural references, subtle humour, and nuance. Speak exactly as a well-educated native speaker would.
